Constant reiteration of mock surprise at depravity is integrated in the architecture that aids and encourages the depraved.
Expressing surprise at the description of horrors that have occurred one's entire lifetime is a mechanism to alleviate the conscience.
These ten-second outrages allow the self-righteous moment needed to push unpleasant data back into warm amnesiac fog, having dealt with it.
The next revelations of the same or similar abuses will again be the first. Things only just now turned frightening. The past is erased.
The cycle continues. Abuse is fleetingly, superficially acknowledged. Its persistence must never be.
